summary: Define scope safely with include/exclude patterns in contexts.
examples: Exclude /logout or /admin if it is out of scope.
Create a context for a single subdomain.
diagrams: Scope boundary: in-scope vs out-of-scope URLs
content:
## Overview
Contexts define scope for scanning and for authentication settings. Use narrow include rules and explicit excludes.

### Best practices
- Prefer explicit include paths
- Use exclude rules for logouts and destructive endpoints
- Validate your regex with sample URLs
title: Guide 2: Contexts and Scope
steps: Create a new context for your target.
Add include paths using regex that match your target URLs.
Add exclude paths for areas you must not scan.
Switch to Protected mode to enforce scope.
Verify scope by opening a few URLs and checking in-scope status.
